author: Mero CMS

posts.title: Getting Started with Mero CMS
posts.excerpt: Learn how to install Mero CMS, run the setup wizard, and go live with your first site in under 10 minutes.
posts.content:
What is Mero CMS?

Mero CMS is an open-source, self-hosted content management system built on NestJS and Next.js. It ships with a module-first architecture — you enable only the features you need.

Prerequisites

  • Node.js 18+
  • PostgreSQL database
  • Git

Installation

Clone the repository and run the setup wizard:

git clone https://github.com/your-org/mero-cms
cd mero-cms
npm install
npm run dev

Then open http://localhost:3000/setup and follow the wizard to create your admin account and choose your modules.

Next Steps

Once setup is complete, install a theme from the Themes page in your dashboard. The theme's seed data will automatically populate your content.

posts.title: How the Module System Works
posts.excerpt: Mero CMS only loads the NestJS modules and database tables that you actually need. Here's how it works under the hood.
posts.content:
Module-First Architecture

Most CMS platforms create every database table and register every route on install — even the ones you'll never use. Mero CMS takes a different approach.

When you complete the setup wizard, you select the modules your project needs. The system then:

  1. Assembles a minimal Prisma schema from just those modules
  2. Runs prisma db push to create only the needed tables
  3. Writes ENABLED_MODULES to the environment and restarts
  4. On restart, NestJS only registers routes for enabled modules

Available Modules

Core modules (always on): Auth, Users, Roles, Settings, Media.

Optional modules: Blogs, Categories, Tags, Comments, Projects, Team, Services, Testimonials, Timeline, Menus, Pages, Leads, SEO, Redirects, Analytics, Themes.

Adding Modules Later

Go to Settings → Modules in your dashboard to enable additional modules. The system will update the schema and restart automatically.

posts.title: Building a Custom Theme for Mero CMS
posts.excerpt: Step-by-step guide to creating a new theme with Next.js 15, connecting it to the CMS API, and publishing seed content.
posts.content:
Theme Structure

A Mero CMS theme is simply a Next.js application that reads content from the CMS public API. Themes live in the themes/ directory of your project.

Required Files

  • theme.json — metadata and seed data
  • src/lib/cms.ts — typed CMS client
  • .env.local — CMS_API_URL pointing to your backend

theme.json Seed Data

Include a seed object in your theme.json to pre-populate the CMS when a user clicks Setup Theme:

{
  "name": "My Theme",
  "requiredModules": ["blogs", "menus"],
  "seed": {
    "menus": [...],
    "posts": [...],
    "testimonials": [...]
  }
}

Media Files

Place images and videos in media/ inside your theme directory. When a user runs Theme Setup, all files in that folder are copied to the CMS media library automatically.

testimonials.content: Mero CMS cut our client onboarding time in half. We set up only the modules each client needs, and the theme system makes deploying a polished frontend trivial.
Finally, a CMS that doesn't lock you into a bloated admin full of features nobody asked for. The modular setup wizard is exactly what agencies need.
The RBAC system and audit logs gave our enterprise clients exactly the compliance controls they needed. Highly recommend for professional deployments.
